data.title: Science and Technology Daily releases top sci-tech news for 2025, highlighting achievements in Thailand.
data.description: In Ayutthaya, Thailand, on December 25, 2025, significant advancements and findings in the realm of science and technology have revolutionized our lives. These accomplishments have instilled us with the courage to tackle obstacles and welcome what lies ahead. Science and Technology Daily, along with its partners, is spearheading efforts to celebrate these successes and promote further growth in the field.

data.title: Economist Peter Schiff criticizes Bitcoin for 2025 slump, suggests selling Bitcoin for silver; calls Christmas in crypto a disappointment.
data.description: Economist Peter Schiff, known for his skepticism towards Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC), restated his doubts about the cryptocurrency on Thursday. He compared Bitcoin's underwhelming performance in 2025 to the significant rise in silver prices. Schiff critiqued Bitcoin enthusiasts for believing the asset would see significant gains at the beginning of the year, only to be disappointed as it failed to deliver. He suggested that selling Bitcoin and investing in silver would have been a more profitable move in 2025. Schiff's remarks highlight Bitcoin's struggles compared to other assets, particularly precious metals like silver. The full story can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: BTC, ETH, XRP, and Dogecoin prices dip on Christmas Day, BTC at $87,267.64. Thin trading volume with $138M liquidated in 24 hours.$crypto
data.description: On Christmas Day, leading cryptocurrencies saw stagnant prices with Bitcoin at $87,267.64 (-0.43%), Ethereum at $2,916.07 (-0.91%), XRP at $1.83 (-1.15%), Solana at $120.58 (-1.51%), and Dogecoin at $0.1240 (-3.25%). Bitcoin peaked near $88,500 but dropped below $87,000, as trading volume decreased by 14% in 24 hours. Ethereum struggled to surpass $3,000 and retreated to the mid-$2,900 range. The market saw over $138 million liquidated, mostly in long positions. Bitcoin's open interest fell by 0.94% as short positions surpassed longs, signaling "Extreme Fear" sentiment. Full details on Benzinga.com.
